value.reason: The cited page explicitly documents a "Scheduled" trigger type for Claude Code Routines: "Scheduled: run on a recurring cadence like hourly, nightly, or weekly, or once at a specific future time." This matches the cell's label ("Schedule Trigger") and description (recurring cadence or once at a specific future time, no human supervision). The page is about Routines broadly, but it specifically and clearly documents the scheduled/triggered run capability as a shipped (research preview) feature. The description's claim of "no human supervision required" is confirmed by the page: "Routines execute on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure" and "Routines run autonomously as full Claude Code cloud sessions: there is no permission-mode picker and no approval prompts during a run." The cell is also distinct from the adjacent capabilities: the cloud execution environment and background/async execution are documented on the same page but the cell specifically targets the scheduling/trigger mechanism, which is what the page primarily describes under the "Scheduled" trigger bullet. No invented claims are present.
